description: Fix three code examples in plugin-class.mdx that are missing the `override` keyword on optional properties — these examples fail to compile with `noImplicitOverride: true` in the scaffolded tsconfig.json. Also expand the existing `override` explanation in plugin-development.mdx to cover optional properties (configSchema, excludePatterns, homepage, teardown), not just lifecycle hooks.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix missing `override` keyword in plugin-class.mdx optional property examples
userStories.description: Three code examples in docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx show optional plugin properties without the required `override` keyword. All three fail to compile with TS4114 when copied into a scaffolded plugin (which uses `noImplicitOverride: true` in tsconfig.json). Fix the examples and add a sentence explaining the requirement.
acceptanceCriteria: A sentence in the Optional Properties section explains that all optional properties require the `override` keyword due to `noImplicitOverride: true` in the scaffolded tsconfig.json
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx (fix three code examples + add one sentence)

Context:
The `OpenTabsPlugin` base class defines optional properties (`excludePatterns`, `homepage`, `configSchema`) and an optional method (`teardown()`). Because the scaffolded tsconfig.json has `noImplicitOverride: true`, any subclass that implements these optional members must use the `override` keyword. Omitting it causes TS4114: 'This member must have an override modifier because it overrides a member in the base class'.

The lifecycle-hooks.mdx page correctly explains this and uses `override` throughout. But plugin-class.mdx has three broken examples in the 'Optional Properties' section.

Changes:
1. Line 117: `readonly excludePatterns = ['*://docs.example.com/*'];`
   → `override readonly excludePatterns = ['*://docs.example.com/*'];`

2. Line 127: `readonly homepage = 'https://github.com';`
   → `override readonly homepage = 'https://github.com';`

3. Line 138: `readonly configSchema: ConfigSchema = {`
   → `override readonly configSchema: ConfigSchema = {`

4. After the table in the 'Optional Properties' section (before '**Example — excluding URLs:**' at line 112), add a sentence: "Because the scaffolded `tsconfig.json` enables `noImplicitOverride`, implementations must use the `override` keyword — omitting it causes a TS4114 type error."

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the abstract properties (name, description, displayName, urlPatterns, tools) — those do not need `override` because they are declared `abstract` on the base class, not optional.
- Do NOT change any prose text other than adding the one explanatory sentence.
- Do NOT change the lifecycle-hooks.mdx — it is already correct.

userStories.title: Expand `override` explanation in plugin-development.mdx to cover optional properties
userStories.description: The plugin-development.mdx guide currently mentions that lifecycle hooks require `override` but doesn't tell developers about the same requirement for optional properties (`configSchema`, `excludePatterns`, `homepage`) and the optional method `teardown()`. Expand the existing sentence to cover all optional members.
acceptanceCriteria: The `override` mention in plugin-development.mdx explicitly includes optional properties (configSchema, excludePatterns, homepage) and teardown() in addition to lifecycle hooks
The explanation still references `noImplicitOverride` so developers understand the source of the TS4114 error
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx (update one sentence)

Context:
Line 105 of plugin-development.mdx currently reads:
"Lifecycle hooks (`onActivate`, `onDeactivate`, `onNavigate`, `onToolInvocationStart`, `onToolInvocationEnd`) require `override` because they are optional methods defined on the base class (TypeScript's `noImplicitOverride` rule enforces this). See [Lifecycle Hooks](/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks) for usage examples."

This is misleading because it implies only lifecycle hooks need `override`. In fact, ALL optional properties and methods on OpenTabsPlugin need `override`: excludePatterns, homepage, configSchema, and teardown().

Changes:
Replace the existing sentence at line 105 with an expanded version that covers all optional members. For example:
"The scaffolded `tsconfig.json` enables `noImplicitOverride`, which means any optional property or method defined on `OpenTabsPlugin` must use the `override` keyword — omitting it causes a TS4114 type error. This applies to lifecycle hooks (`onActivate`, `onDeactivate`, `onNavigate`, `onToolInvocationStart`, `onToolInvocationEnd`), the optional method `teardown()`, and optional properties (`configSchema`, `excludePatterns`, `homepage`). See [Lifecycle Hooks](/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks) for usage examples."

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other part of plugin-development.mdx.
- Do NOT remove the link to lifecycle-hooks.
